title: lonicera caerulea subsp. hirsuta
intro: Lonicera caerulea subsp. hirsuta, also known as the hairy blue honeysuckle, is a subspecies of flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Eastern Europe and Asia. The medicinal properties of Lonicera caerulea subsp. hirsuta lie in its berries, leaves, and roots, which contain various bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce blood sugar levels, lower cholesterol, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, as well as its antioxidant and anti-aging properties. The herbal preparations of Lonicera caerulea subsp. hirsuta include teas, infusions, and tinctures made from the plant's berries, leaves, and roots, which can be consumed for medicinal purposes.
